title: 2×CTAB Protocol for Isolation of predominantly Symbiodinium DNA from symbiotic anemones
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4kprb","text":"This protocol is kit-free and can be used to isolate high quality genomic DNA of predominantly Symbiodinium from symbiotic Aiptasia which can be used as e.g. PCR template for genotyping.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":95,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5uf2l","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ee2vf","text":" It is based on the method described in Coffroth et al., 1992.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3aocn","text":"Make sure to use Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) which is buffered with TE to a neutral to slightly basic pH (everthing starting from 6.7 should work) since DNA stability decreases at acidic pH (the opposite is true for RNA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"42gu5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f6pfr","text":"Be sure not to exceed the maximal ×g for which your microcentrifuge tubes are certified otherwise they might get damaged during centrifugation and release all the hazardous substances (and the DNA of course) into the rotor. You don't want that!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"36t1c","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1skop","text":"If making your own stock solutions of RNase A from powder be aware that some products contain DNase impurities. If this should be the case you need to boil the stock solutions once for 15-20 min at 100°C and let cool down at room temperature to inactivate the DNases. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

100 ml.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":7,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1jmd5","text":"Warm to 65°C under stirring to bring the CTAB into solution.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7snka","text":"Once dissolved, bring final volume to 100 ml using a graduated cylinder.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b8ci6","text":"Filter sterilize (0.2 µm) into sterile 50 ml Falcon tubes and store at −20°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9666l","text":"Heat to 65°C before usage since freezing leads precipitation of the CTAB.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"2d2u2","text":"Safety Remarks: Be sure to follow chemical safety procedures. β-Mercaptoethanol and Phenol are very toxic if inhaled, ingested or by skin contact. So, read the safety data sheets, work under a hood for at least the Phenol and Chloroform steps, wear nitrile gloves (don’t re-use them), safety glasses and a lab coat. If you never worked with Phenol:Chloroform before, ask someone who did about handling of it!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":265,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c5rb5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2mkoi","text":"Waste Disposal: Follow guidelines of your intitution for disposal. E.g. discard bottom organic layers in liquid Phenol-Chloroform waste container, the tubes and glass beads into solid Phenol-Chloroform waste container. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Homogenize anemone with a rotor-stator in 500 µl 0.01% SDS individually in screw-cap or safe-lock 1.5-2 ml microcentrifuge tubes. This is important for the Phenol extraction; simple tubes might leak!Make sure your tubes tolerate the chemicals and centrifugation forces!

Do 5 rounds of sheering through a 25-gauge needle. Important: DNA in host-algae homogenate in SDS or water will degrade rapidly with every freeze-thaw cycle! If you want to perform DNA isolation from these samples later, freeze the samples after step 6.
Pellet algae at 10,000 ×g for 5-10 min.
Remove supernatant and resuspend in 500 µl 0.01% SDS.
Pellet and remove supernatant as before.

Resuspend the pellet in 600 µl 2× CTAB buffer. The 2-Mercaptoethanol in the CTAB buffer is toxic if inhaled, swallowed or at skin contact. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30038887&name=2-Mercaptoethanol
Add ~200 µl of glass beads (0.5 mm).
Vortex for 1-5 min at ≥2000 rpm until no cell clumps are visible.
Add 3.6 µl of Proteinase K (20 mg/ml). Mix by inverting several times.

Incubate at 65°C for 30-60 min. Invert occasionally while incubating. The suspension should become green and less opaque when the cells lyse. 20 min should be enough for less dense samples. 00:30:00, 65°C incubation

Add 600 µl Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, TE-buffered to pH ≥ 7, very TOXIC!!! ), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. This should result in a milky emulsion with two phases starting to form. Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture
Centrifuge for 10 min at 14,000 ×g to separate the phases.
Take ~550 µl of the aqueous, upper phase without disturbing the interphase and transfer to a new tube.

Add 500 µl of Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. You can also use plain chloroform. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148458&name=Chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture
Centrifuge for 10 min at 14,000 ×g.
Take ~450 µl of the aqueous, upper phase and transfer to a new tube. Do NOT disturb the interphase!
Add 1 ml of 100% ethanol (molecular biology grade) and mix well.
Incubate for ≥30 min at room temperature (RT). 00:30:00, Incubation at room temperature
Centrifuge for ≥30 min at 14,000 ×g at RT. Be sure to orient all tubes in the same direction to know on which side the pellet will form.

Decant supernatant. When doing this protocol from a dense culture, you might see a tiny pellet, but probably not if starting with symbiotic anemones. Just be careful to not scratch off the DNA from the side where the pellet should be.
Add 500 µl 70% ethanol, don’t mix, centrifuge for 5 min at 14,000 ×g.
Remove the supernatant carefully with a pipet, without disturbing the pellet. You can also use TE (Tris-EDTA) buffer but be aware that the EDTA might interfere with downstream enzymatic reactions.
Air-dry for 10 min or until no ethanol is visible. 00:10:00, Drying
Add 30 µl ddH 2 O or 10 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.5 to the pellet. You can also use TE (Tris-EDTA) buffer but be aware that the EDTA might interfere with downstream enzymatic reactions.
Store the samples at −20°C indefinitely or use them directly.

Add 8 µl of RNase A (10 mg/ml stock concentration) to the sample, mix well and incubate at 37°C for 30 min. During this step the RNA from the sample is degraded and removed during the next steps. If you started with a lot of sample material, prolong this step to 1 h. 00:30:00, RNase A treatment

Add 600 µl Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, TE-buffered to pH ≥ 7, very TOXIC!!! ), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture
Centrifuge for 10 min at 14,000 ×g to separate the phases.
Take ~500 µl of the aqueous, upper phase without disturbing the interphase and transfer to a new tube.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
